thread.title: 162 votes: Victory margin for lone AIMIM winning candidate in Maharashtra polls
thread.title_full: 162 votes: Victory margin for lone AIMIM winning candidate in Maharashtra polls

author: @indiatoday
title: 162 votes: Victory margin for lone AIMIM winning candidate in Maharashtra polls
text:
162 votes: Victory margin for lone AIMIM winning candidate in Maharashtra polls
Mufti Mohammad Ismail won 1,09,653 votes, defeating Asif Sheikh Rashid of the Indian Secular Largest Assembly of Maharashtra, who got 1,09,491 votes.
In Short
- Mufti Mohammad Ismail wins Malegaon Central Assembly seats
- Ismail polls 1,09,653 votes, defeats Asif Sheikh Rashid
- Asaduddin Owaisi's party contests 14 seats in Maharashtra, loses on 13
Just 162 votes was all that saved All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) candidate Mufti Mohammad Ismail from a defeat in the Malegaon Central Assembly seat of Maharashtra, election results announced on Saturday showed.
While the Asaduddin Owaisi-led party, who fielded 14 candidates, managed to open its account in Maharashtra, its lone winning candidate secured a victory with a narrow margin as he polled 1,09,653 votes, defeating Asif Sheikh Rashid of the Indian Secular Largest Assembly of Maharashtra. Rashid got 1,09,491 votes.
Nihal Ahmed of the Samajwadi Party was third in line with just 9,624 votes and Ejaz Beg of Congress managed 7,527 votes. Interestingly, nine of the candidates vying for the Malegaon Central seat got fewer votes than None of The Above (NOTA). A total of 1,089 votes were cast in favour of NOTA.
Meanwhile, former AIMIM MP Imtiaz Jaleel lost the Aurangabad East seat. BJP's Atul Save defeated Jaleel by just 2,161 votes.
Atul Save received 93,274 votes while Jaleel got 91,113 votes. On the other hand, Waris Pathan remained in fifth position in Bhiwandi West seat. He got only 15,800 votes. Mahesh Prabhakar Chaugule of the BJP won the Bhiwandi West seat with 70,172 votes.
Asaduddin Owaisi's vote share was a meagre 0.85 per cent.
HOW OTHER AIMIM CANDIDATES FARED IN MAHARASHTRA ELECTIONS
Fayaz Ahmed from Byculla lost with 5,531 votes.
Farooq Anwar lost from Dhule city. He got 70,788 votes.
Nasruddin Taqiuddin Siddiqui lost from Aurangabad Central, polling 77,340 votes.
Sarfaraz Khan was defeated in Mumbra Kalwa. He received 13,519 votes.
Raees Lashkaria, who got only 2937 votes, lost from Versova.
Farooq Maqbool lost from Solapur, with 61,428 votes.
Mahesh Kamble lost from Miraj seat. He got 2,599 votes.
Sramat Survade, who was contesting from Murtizapur, got 3201 votes.
Syed Moin was fielded in Nanded South. He got 15,396 votes.
Asma Shaikh lost from Kurla, winning 3,945 votes.
Mohammad Yusuf lost from Karanja. He got 31,042 votes.
MAHARASHTRA ASSEMBLY ELECTION RESULTS
The BJP-led Mahayuti alliance, also comprising Chief Minister Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena and Ajit Pawar-led faction of the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) [registered a landslide victory in the Maharashtra Assembly elections](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/maharashtra-jharkhand-assembly-election-results-counting-of-votes-latest-updates-mahayuti-mva-bjp-jmm-congress-2638056-2024-11-23), winning 233 seats of the 288 seats.
Meanwhile, the Maha Vikas Aghadi (MVA) of the Congress, Shiv Sena (UBT) and Sharad Pawar faction of NCP settled with just 49 seats.
The single-phase elections in Maharashtra were held on November 20.
